title: Why I'm moving on from just aesthetics. Revealing one of the hardest times of my life.
filename: Why I_m moving on from just aesthetics. Revealing one of the hardest times of my life..en.vtt
excerpt: Why I'm moving on from Aesthetics the story is actually very personal to me and it actually started before I even got into medical school on holiday with my family and I took this textbook with me on holiday that's how Keen I was and unfortunately my mom at the time was uh experi…

segments.text: why I'm moving on from Aesthetics the story is actually very personal to me and it actually started before I even got into medical school on holiday with my family and I took this textbook with me on holiday that's how Keen I was and unfortunately my mom at the time was uh experiencing some minor health issues nothing that we were particularly worried about and I started to read this textbook wondering about the sorts of things that it might be and it was actually on this holiday that I realized something really awful um which was while we were walking home one night from a night out and I kept looking back and thinking why is my mom taking so long to walk up this hill and I was actually getting frustrated and I've been reading the textbook that day and I was walking next to Miranda and I saw her silhouette down this long hill in France slightly stooped over and shuffling and I I knew it right then that she had Parkinson's disease and it was yeah it was really

hard for me cuz I couldn't tell anyone else because I didn't want to upset her and she didn't know and my granded had it so we knew as a family how bad things would get I'd lived for 5 years as a child I'd helped care for my nan um when she stayed with us and uh and I knew that was going to happened to my mom but I didn't want her to know a day sooner than it had to be because there's nothing you can really do about it but this unfortunately then unraveled over the next 20 years I did as many things as I could to help my mom first we got married a bit sooner um and then the second awful thing happened on my wedding night which was that she broke her pelvis while dancing uh towards the end of the night so she had an awful combination of Parkinson's disease and osteoporosis and my mom who'd always been the biggest energy in my life was basically slowly dismantled by these two diseases she broke bones every six months or so she was in and out of wheelchairs and she suffered tremendously before dying when she was about 67 from having to see my mom go

through this and slowly deteriorate and do as many things I could to try and give her as much of a life as I could as being a son so I had my children a bit earlier got married a bit earlier all these things ultimately the biggest impact was when I was about 35 she suddenly crashed and got a lot worse um all of a sudden and it was actually only then that I think it really sunk in how bad things were going to get and also uh how this actually imp had implications for me and my family as well and that was something dropped inside me when I suddenly realized bad things do not just happen to other people they happen to me they happen to people like me it was a world shift that suddenly meant like this thing called life this very precious thing that we all enjoy so much it is fleeting and you are out of control of a huge amount of it and for years before then I'd had on my to-do list find out how to prevent Parkinson's I literally wrote it on my Google keep and I used to research and occasionally Google see if anything came up and you get things like get have a

Helo Bop pylori test and maybe it's bacteria and maybe it's pesticides which you can do almost nothing about it's really hard to eat everything organic um and I used to just wheel spin over this in 2015 I had a major anxiety uh situation relating to it as my mom got worse I suddenly realized that I was vulnerable too and I when I was injecting one day I saw a little shake and I'd always known I was a very stable injector I was told that on the first day I ever I was ever injecting oh you're very stable so it kind of got built into me that I was and as I was looking down and I saw the shake this thought just entered my head of like it could be you and you could be going down this route and I had two very young children at that time very vulnerable part of life as well and it just set me in this Loop of thinking about what would happen if it happened to me next um and not being able to have a test for it there's no test for it so I just got stuck worrying tremendously I had about four to six months of ridiculous anxiety you wouldn't tell by

looking at me everything carried on normal but I had huge stress I got all sorts of physical symptoms through anxiety like burning in my chest tinitus which has never really gone away I had palpitations had this burning in my arm from worrying all the time and anyway what that was me coming to terms with the fact that I am as vulnerable as everyone else and then trying to get whatever control I could back and that's really when I started to really obsess over like what can we do to prevent this process that could be leading to me and my family and everyone else get getting these horrible diseases and there was never a solid answer until this new this new Revolution that's starting or called Longevity and there are some key things that have happened that suddenly made me realize that this is where our attention needs to go like there this precious life that we've all been given there are things you can actually do and validate that are going to put you in a way better position to at least get the things when you're much older not when you're my mom was only 8 years older

than I now which doesn't seem like very far off at all so what are the things that you can do now became an obsession and the exciting thing is not only are there things that you can do but there are also tests that you can run that validate that the effort you're putting in is doing something because that's something I always struggled with is how do I know this supplement or this activity or this thing I'm doing is actually making any difference and there's a building there's a building body of that is telling us that this is possible and this is actually where I think the future is lying and I think strangely and ironically it's Medical Aesthetics that might be in a poll position to actually lead some of this change because we have a huge network of clinicians who are willing and able to learn new stuff and to do these tests and to give people the right information and even the interventions that's all going to be based in the private sector because most governments do not think aging is part of disease yet they are going to be reacting to disease for at least another 10 years

while the private sector can be proactively reducing the risk of inting disease and this is tremendously exciting and I think basically the rest of my life is going to be about trying to help clinicians bring the best science forward to help prevent disease and aging so that we actually make our patients younger from the inside it's absolutely happening it's actually here already as they of as is often the case the revolution is already here but most people don't know about it and I would love to play a role in help bringing this information to you guys and to the rest of the world so that we can start to implement changes that really prevent people getting these life ruing diseases so that is my mission for the next well let's hope forever but at least the next 20 to 30 years um and uh and I hope that you'll join me and I hope you're excited about it I think you will be because every time I talk to someone they are excited they're already taking supplements they're already trying to look after their health they're already aware of these things but the problem is we don't have people we can trust teaching us this stuff there's too many people sending mixed

messages and that's something I'd love to contribute to is what is the evidence-base that actually allows you to intervene in a way that is minimal risk maximum benefit and that you believe really will work rather than just trying random things that change and shift with a breeze so if you want to get onto this train the longevity train is about to set off and you want to be involved in the first wave make sure you comment longevity below and we will send you the link
